text: Solar cells based on alkyl ammonium metal halides as photoactive materials have recently shown promising combination of high power conversion efficiency and facile fabrication, pointing towards low-cost thin film solar cells. However, the stability of these solar cells has been a matter of concern and they show significant degradation under sunlight exposure. Here, we report that this degradation is almost quasi-reversible and could be highly suppressed by some minor adjustments in operating conditions of solar cells. The perovskite solar cells using methylammonium (MA)-, formamidinium (FA)-, and mixture of MA(x)FA(1-x) lead triiodide as active material were characterized using the electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS). The variable temperature EIS has shown that the diffusion coefficient of MA ions is much higher than that of FA ions. Powder X-ray diffraction (PXRD) has also confirmed structural change and lattice expansion in MAPbI3 upon heating to operating temperatures. Based on our EIS and PXRD results, we inferred that the enhanced ion movement (fast ion transport) in MAPbI3 under solar light illumination that triggers the thermally activated lattice-expanding phase transition. This effect is attributed to absorption of infrared component of the sun light. Hence, stable solar cell operation lifetimes and under white-light emitting diodes, or under a solar simulator with an infrared cutoff filter or cooling conditions.
explanation: The passage reads as technical and coherent, but includes awkward phrasing and a fragmented ending, which are common in AI-generated or AI-edited text. These inconsistencies raise the likelihood of AI authorship.
